Implemented auto-detect routing in extract(). New signature: extract(pdf_path, *, extractor='auto'|'docling'|'mineru'). Auto mode: fast Docling → if formulas → MinerU → enriched Docling → PyMuPDF fallback chain. 48/48 tests pass, backward compatible.
Threaded extractor param through _pdf_chunks, index_pdf, batch_index_pdfs. All default to 'auto' for backward compat. Fixed existing batch test mock to accept new kwarg. 49/49 tests pass.

Shipped in v4.6.5 (commit fc65747) — MinerU-failed fallback now replays on_page from fast_result.page_boundaries. Regression tests in tests/test_pdf_extractor.py::TestAutoDetectRouting cover multi-page replay + on_page=None contract (verified to fail pre-fix with 'expected 3 callbacks, got 0'; pass post-fix). Follow-up niceties in the bead body (helper extraction, _progress line on fallback) can land as separate cleanup if desired; the core bug is fixed and released.

Root cause fixed in v4.6.4 (commit 9696cc5) — start_new_session=True + os.killpg at shutdown so resource_tracker runs sem_unlink cleanly on MinerU worker teardown. Empirically verified 2026-04-18: full 15-paper Delos re-index (2007 chunks, ~25 min continuous MinerU server operation) completed with POSIX sem namespace at full 9990-slot headroom throughout. nx doctor --check-resources now surfaces pressure early. Repro script preserved at ~/nexus-blog/repro/mineru_sem_leak.py for future regression verification.

Resolved downstream by v4.6.4's killpg cleanup (commit 9696cc5). Root cause was the same pattern nexus-ze2a described — nx_tidy spawns claude -p, which loads its own nexus MCP server, which previously leaked POSIX semaphores and exhausted the parent's namespace. Verified 2026-04-18 post-4.6.5: mcp__plugin_nx_nexus__nx_tidy(topic='...', collection='knowledge__nexus') ran to completion without disconnecting the MCP server. No separate fix needed.

Resolved. Root-cause fix in v4.6.4 (commit 9696cc5, "process-group
cleanup for T1 chroma + MinerU"):
  - B3 addressed: session.py:281 now spawns chroma with
    start_new_session=True; stop_t1_server uses
    killpg(getpgid(pid), SIGTERM→SIGKILL) so chroma's multiprocessing
    workers and resource_tracker children receive the signal and
    sem_unlink() before exit.
  - B6 root cause eliminated: no orphan Python resource_tracker /
    spawn_main processes observed at close time (verified via
    `ps ... | awk '$2==1'` — empty).
  - One-time cleanup: 333 orphan chromas mass-reaped 2026-04-17.

Current state (2026-04-18): 26 orphan chromas with ppid=1 remain,
accumulation rate has dropped from ~95/day (pre-fix) to a trickle
consistent with Claude Code sessions that crash-exit before their
atexit teardown. Semaphore exhaustion symptom (the joblib warning)
has not recurred since the v4.6.4 mass-reap.

Defense-in-depth items B1/B2/B4/B5 remain useful but are lower
priority now that the leak RATE is bounded. Filed separately if
pursued:
  - B1: nx/hooks/scripts/session_end_hook.py to invoke the existing
    session_end() from Claude SessionEnd.
  - B2: extend sweep_stale_sessions to detect unowned chroma
    processes by command-line pattern, ignoring the 24h age gate
    for ppid=1 orphans.
  - B4: atexit.register(stop_t1_server, ...) in the MCP server
    bootstrap + any nx CLI that starts its own T1 server.
  - B5: sweep validates the session file's parent PID is alive;
    reap session file + child server when not.

Resolved in commit 88933bb (PR #173), shipped in v4.7.0.

Fix: src/nexus/doc/resolvers.py:172-187 — RdrResolver._fetch now
iterates self._rdr_dir.glob('*.md') and matches via re.IGNORECASE
instead of case-sensitive Path.glob('rdr-{key}-*.md'). Numeric keys
still match any zero-padding of the same integer; non-numeric keys
use re.escape for safe literal matching.

Tests: 4 in tests/test_rdr_082_doc_tokens.py::TestRdrResolver all
green:
  - test_resolves_uppercase_RDR_prefix
  - test_mixed_case_cohabitation
  - test_zero_padding_still_works_case_insensitive
  - test_non_numeric_key_case_insensitive

Live smoke (uppercase RDR-068-*.md + {{rdr:068.status}} token):
  nx doc render test.md
  → rendered 1 tokens across 1 file(s)
  → "RDR-068 status: accepted"

Original bead repro (rendered 0 tokens, "no file matching" error) no
longer reproduces.

Error message also improved: now says "case-insensitive" explicitly
so future users see that case is not the issue.

RDR-064 nx console shipped and MVV-verified.

Shipping evidence:
  - src/nexus/console/ package — app.py, config.py, watchers.py
    + routes/ (activity, campaigns, health, partials) + templates +
    static/ assets (htmx, alpine, pico, console.css). Total ~357 LOC.
  - src/nexus/commands/console.py — nx console CLI entry with
    --host / --port flags, PID-file lifecycle, stale-PID reap on
    startup (v4.7.0 remediation), 0o600 PID file perms.
  - Unit tests: 15/15 green in tests/test_console_app.py +
    tests/test_console_deps.py (activity/health/campaigns page
    renders, static asset serving, scope param defaults,
    FastAPI/uvicorn/sse-starlette importability).

Live MVV (sandboxed NEXUS_CONFIG_DIR + NX_LOCAL=1, ephemeral port):
  - nx console --port <ephemeral> --host 127.0.0.1 binds and
    serves in ~3.5s.
  - curl http://127.0.0.1:$PORT/activity → HTTP 200 in 0.6ms.
  - Clean shutdown on SIGTERM; PID file reaped.

Phase 0 (logging prereq) + Phase 1 (v0.1 panels: activity, sessions,
health, campaigns) are in the codebase and tested. No deferred work
remains in this epic — RDR-064's scope is complete.

Shipped in bb29245.

Surfaces:
- nx collection health: new chash_indexed_ratio column; bottom-of-
  report hint when any row ratio < 1.0 suggesting
  `nx collection backfill-hash --all`.
- nx collection audit: new "chash_index coverage" section 5 with
  total_chunks, indexed_rows, ratio + best-effort list of up to 5
  unindexed chunk IDs (bounded T3 get + T2 cross-check).

Data flow: _default_chash_coverage_fn reads chash_index under
ChashIndex._lock and divides by T3 col.count(). Returns None when
either side unreachable. No new primitives — pure SQL composition
of the RDR-086 chash_index surface and the existing T3 wrapper.

Tests: 8 new (4 health + 4 audit) covering ratio=1.0, 0 < ratio < 1.0
with missing sample, empty collection, missing T2. 198 regression
tests green across collection/chash/resolve/phase4-5 suites.

Next: let a cycle of real usage show whether the hint text format
works; rename / copy (nexus-1ccq) naturally extends this surface
since any rename needs to update both T2 (chash_index
physical_collection column) and the surfaces we just added.

Fixed via _select_primary_rdr disambiguation in src/nexus/doc/resolvers.py. Primary RDR files win over child artifacts (IMPL / post-mortem / supplement / calibration). Deterministic filesystem-independent sort. 6 regression tests + live smoke on ART RDR-072 repro. Pushed as commit that follows bb29245 on main.

Regression test added at tests/test_catalog_isolation.py (5 tests). The underlying leak was fixed 2026-04-08 by RDR-060 (autouse _isolate_catalog fixture in tests/conftest.py) — no new int-cce-* owners have been written to the user's real catalog since. These tests lock the behaviour in: they exercise the historical leak pattern (index_markdown with corpus=int-cce-<uid>), verify the hook's is_initialized() guard fires, and assert the real catalog's mtime is unchanged for the test duration. The 64 pre-existing orphans are handled by nexus-tmbh (dedupe-owners backfill).

Enforcement added to Catalog.register_owner() at src/nexus/catalog/catalog.py — raises ValueError when owner_type='repo' and repo_hash is empty/whitespace. Audit of src/nexus/ call sites: all repo-type sites (indexer.py:264, commands/catalog.py:1180) already call owner_for_repo(repo_hash) first and fall through only on miss, passing repo_hash explicitly. Curator sites (doc_indexer.py, pipeline_stages.py, commands/store.py, commands/catalog.py:1123) don't claim owner_type='repo' — untouched. Tests: 3 new tests in TestRegisterOwner (reject empty, reject whitespace, allow non-repo types). 451 catalog-related tests pass.

Added alias_of column to documents schema (with auto-migration for existing DBs), alias_of field on DocumentRecord and CatalogEntry dataclasses. New Catalog methods: resolve_alias(tumbler, max_hops=16) walks the chain with cycle protection, set_alias(tumbler, canonical) writes the pointer (rejects self-aliases), and resolve(tumbler, follow_alias=True) transparently dereferences. Dangling targets terminate at the last valid hop rather than raising, so reads stay available. 8 new tests in TestAliasResolution covering canonical-self, transitive chains, cycles, self-alias rejection, dangling refs, and schema migration. 502 catalog tests pass.

Implemented src/nexus/catalog/dedupe.py (plan_dedupe + apply_alias_plan + apply_remove_plan + apply_plan) and CLI 'nx catalog dedupe-owners' at src/nexus/commands/catalog.py. Classifies curator owners into alias/remove/skip buckets: test-orphan patterns (int-cce-*, int-prov-*, int-*-*, pdf-e2e-*) flagged for removal; synthetic <repo>-<hash> names matching a canonical repo flagged for alias consolidation; everything else skipped for manual review. Apply writes JSONL tombstones for removes and appends updated document records for aliases so rebuilds preserve state. Dry-run is default; --apply commits; --json emits the full plan. 15 new tests in tests/test_catalog_dedupe.py. Verified against real catalog (dry-run): 6 aliasable orphans (258 docs), 67 removable test orphans, 10 legitimate skips. Not applied — user must run 'nx catalog dedupe-owners --apply && nx catalog sync' themselves.

All four workstreams shipped: (1) test isolation regression test at tests/test_catalog_isolation.py locks in the RDR-060 autouse fixture that stopped int-cce-* leakage on 2026-04-08; (2) register_owner(owner_type='repo') now rejects empty repo_hash (src/nexus/catalog/catalog.py) — all 6 audit sites already comply; (3) documents.alias_of column with Catalog.resolve_alias() + set_alias() for transitive, cycle-safe resolution; (4) 'nx catalog dedupe-owners' classifies+applies alias consolidation for synthetic <repo>-<hash> orphans and unconditional removal for test orphans. Dry-run against real catalog projects: 6 aliases (258 docs) + 67 removes (3 pdf-e2e + 64 int-cce) + 10 skips. 28 new tests, 517 catalog tests pass.
